chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI C. P. No. D-7142 of 2022 Date Order with signature of Judge FRESH CASE. 1. For orders on Misc. No.30222/2022. 2. For orders on Misc. No.30223/2022. 3. For orders on Misc. No.30224/2022. 4. For hearing of main case. 22.11.2022. Mr. Muhammad Azam Makhdoom, Advocate for Petitioner. ------- YOUSUF ALI SAYEED, J. - The Petitioner professes to be one of the founders of a Welfare Association said to be registered with Directorate of Social Welfare under the Voluntary Social Welfare Agencies (Registration and Control) Ordinance, 1961 and seeks that the private Respondents No.5 to 7, who are said to be at the helm of the Association, be restrained from inducting fresh members till such time as a new managing body is elected under the bye-laws, with it also being sought that fresh elections be ordered in the interest of justice. However, a perusal of the Petition reflects the pleadings to be couched in vague and general terms, bereft of any relevant grounds, and it merely being averred that the aforesaid Respondents are disturbing and spoiling the work of Association and not complying with the relevant bye-laws. Furthermore, as it transpires the Association itself has not been arrayed as a party to the proceedings and the Petitioners have also not approached the Social Welfare Department through any application/ representation but have merely addressed some correspondence to certain law enforcement agencies/personnel. 2 Needless to say, a dispute between private persons does not constitute a fit case within the parameters of Article 199 of the Constitution, and under the given circumstances, we are of the view that the instant Petition is not maintainable.

However, a perusal of the Petition reflects the pleadings to be couched in vague and general terms, bereft of any relevant grounds, and it merely being averred that the aforesaid Respondents are disturbing and spoiling the work of Association and not complying with the relevant bye-laws. Furthermore, as it transpires the Association itself has not been arrayed as a party to the proceedings and the Petitioners have also not approached the Social Welfare Department through any application/ representation but have merely addressed some correspondence to certain law enforcement agencies/personnel. 2 Needless to say, a dispute between private persons does not constitute a fit case within the parameters of Article 199 of the Constitution, and under the given circumstances, we are of the view that the instant Petition is not maintainable. That being so, while granting the application for urgency, we hereby dismiss the Petition in limine, along with the other miscellaneous applications. JUDGE CHIEF JUSTICE MUBASHIR
